special_requirements.comment: Back Walkover and Back Handspring Series fulfill this requirement.
Split Leap, Full Turn, and Straight Jump fulfill this requirement.
Back Tuck Dismount fulfills this requirement.
Back Walkover, Back Handsprings (within series), and Back Tuck Dismount (C-value) fulfill this requirement.

artistry.notes: The routine lacked consistent fluidity and dynamic expression. Transitions were functional but not always seamless, contributing to a slightly stiff quality of movement. Choreography was typical for the level but did not showcase unique variety.

coaching_summary: The gymnast completed a full JO Level 6 Balance Beam routine, demonstrating proficiency in all required elements. The mount was executed cleanly, and the Back Tuck dismount showed good height. However, the routine accumulated numerous small deductions primarily due to inconsistent body tension (especially in the knees and toes), insufficient split angle on the leap, and frequent balance checks. Landings, particularly the dismount, also require more control to minimize steps and maintain an upright body position. Focusing on these areas will significantly improve the overall score and readiness for higher levels.
top_3_fixes: Maintain Leg Tension: Actively lock knees and point toes throughout all skills, especially during flight, handstand phases (e.g., Back Walkover, Back Handsprings), and jumps. Implement drills like handstand holds with resistance bands around ankles and straight leg lifts with active toe point.
Improve Split Angle: Focus on dynamic flexibility and active hip flexor engagement to achieve the required 150-degree split for dance elements. Incorporate oversplit stretches and dynamic leg swings.

Refine Landings: Practice controlled, stuck landings for all skills, particularly the dismount. Emphasize landing with the chest up, knees slightly bent, and feet together. Utilize dismounts to a firm surface focusing on holding a perfectly stacked, hollow-body position for 3 seconds post-landing.
celebrations: The mount was executed with excellent control and no deductions.
The gymnast successfully completed all required elements for a Level 6 routine.
The Back Tuck dismount showed good height and rotation.
